caseDescription: The brass key to North Quay Animation Workshop's locked armature cage vanished after a sponsor-reel reshoot, and the overtime ledger disappeared with it. Five night-shift crew members remained inside the miniature harbor stage, but only one suspect survives the logic of the valid clue, the planted red herring, and the neutral timing detail.
locationImagePromptBase: empty stop-motion studio lineup setting inside a miniature harbor stage bay, painted quay floor with clear floor plane, model warehouse walls, rigging tables, warm practical lamps, shelves of handcrafted puppets and set crates, enough open space for five standing suspects, no people, no characters
featuredImagePromptBase: after-hours stop-motion harbor set after a theft, empty brass key hook beside the locked armature cage, half-open production drawer, miniature dock scenery under moody studio lamps, no people, physical clue atmosphere
instruction: Study the lineup, compare the interviews, and separate the real clue from the trap: one evidence item truly incriminates, one misleads, and one sharpens the timeline without accusing anyone.
explanation: The guilty suspect is Safiya Noor, slot 3. The valid evidence is Indigo Clay Thread, which places Safiya at the armature cage after the reshoot call because the same indigo harbor clay used to mute the cage hasp is pressed into the inner seam of her leather folio. Brass Grease Nick is the red herring: it points toward Colin Reeve because he regularly services puppet joints and the cage latch, so grease on his cuff comes from routine maintenance rather than the theft. Exposure Slate Dust Arc is neutral evidence; it proves the sponsor reshoot order changed minutes before lights-out and narrows the time window, but it does not accuse anyone by itself. Safiya's paperwork motive, her false claim that she never left the booth, and the valid clay transfer make her the only suspect consistent with the full case.

suspects.dialogue: I was resetting tiny jackets and rig sleeves in wardrobe trays. I never went near the armature cage or the production drawer once reshoots started.
suspects.profile: Age 38, meticulous puppet wardrobe lead who treats every stitched coat and sleeve rig like a museum object under deadline.
suspects.motive: Could want to hide damage to a sponsor puppet costume before the loss reached production accounting.
suspects.notes: Her workbench keeps her anchored in the wardrobe alcove, and cloth fibers do not connect her to the cage or ledger.

visualIdentity.silhouette: layered seamstress apron over a narrow work dress
visualIdentity.wardrobe: dark work dress, pinstriped apron, cuffed sleeves, practical ankle boots
visualIdentity.hair: dark hair in a tidy braided crown
visualIdentity.accessorySignature: yellow pincushion strap at the wrist
visualIdentity.props: miniature coat hanger rail and thread snips

suspects.portraitPromptBase: puppet wardrobe lead, layered seamstress apron over a narrow work dress, practical ankle boots, yellow pincushion strap, miniature coat hanger rail, thread snips, coral spool-thread accent, upright detail-checking stance, full body standing

suspects.dialogue: I greased the gull puppets, checked the cage latch, and locked my tools. If that brass has my grease on it, that's because I service it every week.
suspects.profile: Age 42, blunt armature technician who keeps the studio's puppets moving with careful joints, grease, and metal patience.
suspects.motive: Could want to cover an unauthorized armature swap that would cost the workshop an expensive sponsor shoot day.
suspects.notes: The red herring points naturally toward him because he handles the cage hardware, but the stronger transfer chain stops there.

visualIdentity.silhouette: boxy mechanic jacket with heavy tool apron
visualIdentity.wardrobe: grease-marked work jacket, rolled sleeves, reinforced trousers, heavy lace-up boots
visualIdentity.hair: short sandy hair with rough sideburns
visualIdentity.accessorySignature: steel caliper clipped to the chest pocket
visualIdentity.props: oil rag and puppet-joint wrench set

suspects.portraitPromptBase: armature technician, boxy mechanic jacket with heavy tool apron, rolled sleeves, heavy lace-up boots, steel caliper, oil rag, puppet-joint wrench set, mustard stitch accent, square planted stance, full body standing

suspects.dialogue: I stayed in the booth reconciling sponsor timings and overtime sheets. I never knelt by the harbor crates once the reshoot call came through.
suspects.profile: Age 33, controlled production coordinator who keeps budgets, schedules, and sponsor pressure aligned with unnerving calm.
suspects.motive: Needed to remove the overtime ledger before it exposed falsified reimbursements buried inside her sponsor reconciliations.
suspects.notes: She has the paperwork motive, the authority to touch the ledger, and the only interview statement broken cleanly by the valid transfer clue.

visualIdentity.silhouette: belted office coat over a long narrow skirt line
visualIdentity.wardrobe: tailored office coat, slim gloves, fitted blouse, heeled boots
visualIdentity.hair: dark hair in a polished low braid
visualIdentity.accessorySignature: brass timing stylus tucked beside the collar
visualIdentity.props: structured leather folio and pencil bundle

suspects.portraitPromptBase: production coordinator, belted office coat over a long narrow skirt line, heeled boots, brass timing stylus, structured leather folio, pencil bundle, cobalt file-tab accent, composed half-turn stance, full body standing

suspects.dialogue: I was patching quay stones with fresh clay at the far bench. I shape dock walls for the camera, not ledgers or cage keys.
suspects.profile: Age 36, imaginative set sculptor who can make a tabletop harbor feel weathered, wet, and decades old by eye alone.
suspects.motive: Could want to hide unauthorized material use that pushed the set budget past the sponsor cap.
suspects.notes: He handles the harbor clay openly, but his bench location and tool pattern do not connect him to the missing ledger.

visualIdentity.silhouette: splattered sculptor smock with rolled apron skirt
visualIdentity.wardrobe: dusty studio smock, rolled apron, work trousers, worn boots
visualIdentity.hair: wavy dark hair pushed back messily
visualIdentity.accessorySignature: looped wooden sculpting tool at the hip

suspects.portraitPromptBase: set sculptor, splattered sculptor smock with rolled apron skirt, worn boots, looped wooden sculpting tool, palette knife, clay tray, ochre apron-tie accent, easy off-balance maker stance, full body standing

suspects.dialogue: I pushed the reshoot because the sponsor wanted a cleaner opening move. After that I was on lighting marks, not inside the cage bay.
suspects.profile: Age 35, urgent assistant director who drives miniature crews hard and hates losing momentum to technical excuses.
suspects.motive: Could want to bury an unapproved reshoot order before the producer blamed her for the overtime spike.
suspects.notes: Her pressure shaped the timeline, but the neutral clue only shows when the reshoot changed, not who stole the key and ledger.

visualIdentity.silhouette: cropped director jacket over slim utility trousers
visualIdentity.wardrobe: short studio jacket, slim trousers, ankle boots, rolled sleeves
visualIdentity.hair: copper hair tied in a practical ponytail
visualIdentity.props: shot-list baton and lamp cue cards

suspects.portraitPromptBase: assistant director, cropped director jacket over slim utility trousers, ankle boots, headset loop, whistle chain, shot-list baton, lamp cue cards, crimson lanyard accent, forward-driving stance, full body standing

evidence.title: Brass Grease Nick
evidence.description: Dark joint grease on Colin's cuff appears to match the armature cage latch, making him look like the obvious culprit at first glance.
evidence.imagePromptBase: dark puppet-joint grease across a rolled technician cuff beside a brass cage latch and tiny hex wrench, macro physical clue detail

evidence.title: Indigo Clay Thread
evidence.description: A thin thread of indigo harbor-set clay is pressed into the inner seam of Safiya's leather folio, matching the fresh clay used to mute the cage hasp during the theft.
evidence.imagePromptBase: indigo sculpting clay thread pressed into a leather folio seam beside a brass latch scratch, macro physical clue detail

evidence.title: Exposure Slate Dust Arc
evidence.description: A crescent of lamp dust on the camera slate shelf proves the sponsor reshoot order changed minutes before lights-out, narrowing when the cage could have been opened.
evidence.imagePromptBase: crescent dust arc on a clapper slate shelf beside moved timing pegs and warm lamp dust, contextual studio clue through materials and arrangement
